fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the clamping block should execute, but the inverted condition means it is skipped — the branch condition should be `if clamp` not `if not clamp`.
The else branch (no clamping applied) should only be reached when clamp is False, but due to the inverted condition it is reached when clamp is True.
The then branch (clamping applied) should only be reached when clamp is True, but the inverted condition causes it to execute when clamp is False.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the final y value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the bug causes clamping to be skipped when clamp=True.
